Description & Facts: Magna Carta (Global precursor): Limited monarch's power; early protections against arbitrary arrest/trial, influencing U.S. Bill of Rights.
Political Party Role: N/A
Heroes: King John (forced signer); Barons who demanded reforms.

Description & Facts: Salem Witch Trials: 20 executed, 200+ accused in Puritan Massachusetts; mass hysteria led to spectral evidence trials. Exposed flaws in due process.
Political Party Role: N/A
Heroes: Rev. Increase Mather (criticized trials, helped end them).
Villains: Judges like William Stoughton (enforced spectral evidence); Rev. Samuel Parris (fueled accusations).
Scapegoats: Accused women (e.g., Tituba, an enslaved woman; Bridget Bishop, independent widow) blamed for community ills.

Description & Facts: U.S. Declaration of Independence & Constitution: Declared "all men created equal" but excluded women/slaves; set stage for amendments expanding rights.
Political Party Role: Emerging parties: Federalists (pro-central gov't).

Description & Facts: French Revolution & Declaration of the Rights of Man: Emphasized liberty/equality; inspired U.S. abolitionists.
Political Party Role: N/A

Description & Facts: Slavery & Abolition Movement: Transatlantic slave trade peaked; U.S. enslaved ~4M by 1860.
Political Party Role: Democrats: Pro-slavery South; Whigs/early Republicans: Anti-expansion.
Heroes: Frederick Douglass (escaped slave, orator); Harriet Tubman (Underground Railroad).
Villains: Plantation owners; Fugitive Slave Act enforcers.

Description & Facts: U.S. Civil War & Emancipation Proclamation: War killed 620K; Proclamation freed 3.5M slaves in Confederate states (Jan. 1, 1863).
Political Party Role: Republicans: Led abolition; Democrats: Split (Southern pro-slavery).
Heroes: Lincoln; Union Gen. Ulysses S. Grant.

Description & Facts: 13th–15th Amendments & Reconstruction: Ended slavery; granted citizenship/voting to Black men. ~2K Black officeholders.
U.S. President (if applicable) & Party: Andrew Johnson (D, post-Lincoln); Ulysses S. Grant (R).
Political Party Role: Republicans: Pushed amendments; Democrats: Southern resistance (Black Codes).

Description & Facts: Plessy v. Ferguson: Upheld "separate but equal" segregation; reversed Reconstruction gains.
Political Party Role: Democrats: Southern Jim Crow enforcers; Republicans: Northern support waned.

Description & Facts: 19th Amendment (Women's Suffrage): Women gain vote after Seneca Falls (1848) & decades of activism; global wave post-WWI.
Political Party Role: Democrats: Mixed (Southern opposition); Republicans: Earlier support.
Heroes: Susan B. Anthony; Alice Paul (hunger strikes).

Description & Facts: Holocaust (Global): Nazis murdered 6M Jews, 5M others (Roma, disabled, LGBTQ+, Poles). Key events: Nuremberg Laws (1935); Kristallnacht (1938); Wannsee Conference (1942); Auschwitz liberation (1945).
Political Party Role: U.S. Democrats: Delayed refugee aid (e.g., St. Louis ship turned away, 1939); isolationism. Global: Nazi Party (far-right).
Heroes: Raoul Wallenberg (diplomat, saved 100K Jews); Oskar Schindler.
Scapegoats: Jews (scapegoated for economic woes); LGBTQ+ (pink triangles in camps).

Description & Facts: Executive Order 9981 (Desegregate Military) & UDHR (Global): Truman ends military segregation; UN UDHR sets universal rights standard.
Political Party Role: Democrats: Truman's bold move alienated South.
Scapegoats: Black soldiers (denied equal service in WWII).

Description & Facts: Brown v. Board of Education: Ended school segregation; overturned Plessy.
Political Party Role: Republicans: Eisenhower enforced via troops; Democrats: Southern filibuster.

Description & Facts: Montgomery Bus Boycott: Sparked by Rosa Parks' arrest; 381-day boycott desegregates buses.
Political Party Role: Bipartisan local pressure.

Description & Facts: March on Washington & "I Have a Dream" Speech: 250K march; MLK's speech demands jobs/justice.
Political Party Role: Democrats: JFK proposed civil rights bill; Republicans: Supported.
Villains: FBI Director J. Edgar Hoover (spied on MLK).

Description & Facts: Assassination of JFK & Civil Rights Act: JFK killed Nov. 22; Act bans discrimination in public places/jobs (Jul. 2, 1964).
Political Party Role: Democrats: LBJ (Southern D) pushed it; 80% House Dems yes vs. 60% GOP. Republicans: Key cloture votes.

Description & Facts: Freedom Summer & Voting Rights Act: Murders of activists; Act (Aug. 6, 1965) bans literacy tests, adds oversight.
Political Party Role: Democrats: LBJ signed; Republicans: Co-sponsored. Party realignment begins.

Description & Facts: Selma Marches ("Bloody Sunday"): State troopers attack marchers; leads to Voting Rights Act.
Political Party Role: Bipartisan federal enforcement.

Description & Facts: MLK Assassination & Fair Housing Act: MLK killed Apr. 4; Act bans housing discrimination (Apr. 11). Riots in 100+ cities.
Political Party Role: Democrats: Passed post-assassination; Republicans: Supported.

Description & Facts: Affirmative Action Expansion: Nixon's "Philadelphia Plan" (1969) sets minority hiring goals in federal contracts; strengthened under EEOC.
Political Party Role: Republicans: Nixon supported as economic policy; Democrats: Mixed (unions resisted).

Description & Facts: Roe v. Wade (Women's Rights): Legalized abortion nationwide (overturned 2022); tied to bodily autonomy.
Political Party Role: Republicans: Mixed; Democrats: Pro-choice shift.

Description & Facts: AIDS Crisis & Reagan Response: ~700K U.S. deaths; delayed funding stigmatized LGBTQ+.
Political Party Role: Republicans: Slow response; Democrats: Pushed funding.

Description & Facts: Don't Ask, Don't Tell (LGBTQ+ Military): Allowed gay service if closeted; repealed 2011.
Political Party Role: Democrats: Compromise policy; Republicans: Opposed open service.

Description & Facts: Lawrence v. Texas: Struck down sodomy laws; decriminalized same-sex intimacy.
Political Party Role: Bipartisan ruling.

Description & Facts: Proposition 8 (LGBTQ+ Setback): Banned same-sex marriage in CA; overturned 2015.
U.S. President (if applicable) & Party: George W. Bush (R, prior); Barack Obama (D)
Political Party Role: Republicans: Backed bans; Democrats: Evolving support.

Description & Facts: Affordable Care Act (Affirmative Action Tie-In): Expanded healthcare; indirectly aided minorities via equity focus.
Political Party Role: Democrats: Passed; Republicans: Opposed.

Description & Facts: Defense of Marriage Act Overturned: Windsor v. U.S. struck federal ban; path to Obergefell.
Political Party Role: Democrats: Supported; Republicans: Defended DOMA.

Description & Facts: Obergefell v. Hodges: Nationwide same-sex marriage (Jun. 26).
Political Party Role: Democrats: Celebrated; Republicans: Dissenting justices.

Description & Facts: Black Lives Matter (BLM): Founded post-Trayvon Martin (2013); surged after George Floyd (2020). Protests in 2K+ cities; led to police reforms.
Political Party Role: Democrats: Ally (e.g., Biden's equity exec order); Republicans: "Defund" backlash.
Heroes: Alicia Garza, Patrisse Cullors, Opal Tometi (founders); DeRay Mckesson.
Scapegoats: Black people (e.g., Floyd, scapegoated via "superpredator" myths).

Description & Facts: George Floyd Protests & Global Echoes: 15–26M U.S. participants; influenced global anti-racism (e.g., UK topple of slave trader statues).
Political Party Role: Republicans: "Law/order" response; Democrats: Reform push.

Description & Facts: Executive Order on Advancing Racial Equity: Biden signs EO (Jan. 20) to embed equity in federal policy; reverses Trump-era bans on diversity training.
Political Party Role: Democrats: Pushed equity; Republicans: Criticized as "woke."

Description & Facts: Dobbs v. Jackson: Overturned Roe v. Wade (Jun. 24); states ban abortion, impacting women’s autonomy.
Political Party Role: Democrats: Opposed; Republicans: Celebrated.

Description & Facts: Anti-Trans Legislation Surge: 500+ bills in states (e.g., bathroom bans, sports restrictions); 2024 saw 600+ introduced, per Trans Legislation Tracker.
Political Party Role: Democrats: Opposed; Republicans: Led state-level bans.
